title: Wilms Tumor (Nephroblastoma)
buzzwords: Aggressive malignancy of early childhood
Peak incidence ages 2 to 5
Painless palpable abdominal mass in a toddler
Hematuria and hypertension with unilateral flank mass
Large well-circumscribed renal tumor not crossing midline
Triphasic blastemal, epithelial, and stromal histology
Treated with vincristine, dactinomycin, doxorubicin
Nephroblastoma from WT1/WT2 mutation on chromosome 11

title: WAGR Syndrome
buzzwords: Contiguous gene deletion syndrome with renal tumor risk
Child with absent iris and developmental delay
Cryptorchidism or ambiguous genitalia with kidney tumor predisposition
Genitourinary anomalies plus intellectual disability tetrad
Surveillance ultrasound every 3 months until age 5
Aniridia paired with nephroblastoma
Germline deletion at 11p13 involving WT1
Wilms, Aniridia, GU anomalies, Range of developmental delay

title: Denys-Drash Syndrome
buzzwords: Childhood renal failure with gonadal dysgenesis
Early-onset nephrotic syndrome progressing to chronic kidney disease
Abnormal external genitalia with predisposition to renal tumor
Disorders of sexual development plus nephroblastoma risk
Triad of CKD, DSD, and Wilms tumor
Diffuse mesangial sclerosis on renal biopsy
Germline missense mutation in WT1
Dominant-negative WT1 point mutation syndrome

title: Beckwith-Wiedemann Syndrome
buzzwords: Pediatric overgrowth syndrome with embryonal tumor risk
Newborn with enlarged tongue and abdominal wall defect
Asymmetric body overgrowth with neonatal hypoglycemia
Organomegaly with predisposition to nephroblastoma
Macroglossia, omphalocele, hemihypertrophy triad
Surveillance ultrasound every 3 months until age 7
Imprinting disorder at 11p15.5 (CDKN1C, IGF2)
Beckwith-Wiedemann overgrowth syndrome
